untranslatable_reason: This signal is a measurement framework for calculating annual returns by comparing year-end closing prices, not a trading entry/exit rule. It requires temporal logic to identify last days of December and January across multiple years, establish year-over-year price comparisons, and measure 12-month performance. The allowed operators (>, <, >=, <=, ==, crosses_above, crosses_below, is) and allowed indicators do not support: (1) date-based filtering by month/day boundaries, (2) multi-year lookback comparisons with year-change detection, (3) forward-shifted calendar logic (fwdshift function), or (4) direct year-over-year returns calculation. This is a performance measurement rule, not a buy/sell signal suitable for the backtest engine's condition-based rule structure.
translation_notes: Signal CHAN_QT_DRY_0 defines a framework for calculating annual returns by identifying December year-end dates and January year-end dates, then comparing closing prices across 12-month periods. While 'returns' and 'log_returns' indicators exist, they cannot be conditioned on specific calendar boundaries (last day of December, last day of January) or configured to measure year-over-year changes. The backtest engine requires explicit entry/exit conditions; this signal is a measurement methodology rather than a trading rule.
